novelty_flags.label: Theorem 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The claimed strict tightening against RambachanRoth2023CrediblePT is a different stronger TV class versus a relative-magnitude box, not a strict tightening of the same published assumption.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 3
novelty_flags.one_line: The projection frontier/active-face row-space object is promised but Exhibit 9.2 does not compute the row-space criterion or active TV dual face from the §6 primitives.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 3
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship projection claim cites SunAbraham2021DynamicEffects and BorusyakJaravelSpiess2024EventStudies only as broad comparators, without section/equation/page anchors tying them to the specific aggregation-projection gap.

novelty_flags.label: overall
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; the surviving new content is a routine TV dual norm plus an under-specified projection criterion, not yet a flagship-grade new causal object.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The sign-loss frontier ignores the range cap: if |hat theta| > B, zero never enters the capped interval, but the formula gives a finite kappa_sign.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 3
soundness_flags.one_line: The displayed row-space equality is dimensionally unclear: row(D_agg), row(A), and row(A D_coh) are not defined in compatible ambient spaces under the stated maps.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 3
soundness_flags.one_line: Exhibit 9.2 switches from the locked aggregate target omega' A v to a cohort-specific target loading positively on cohort a and negatively on cohort b.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add the missing boundary case kappa_sign = infinity when |hat theta| > B, or impose |hat theta| <= B explicitly before stating the sign-loss frontier.
Rewrite Conjecture 3 with compatible operator dimensions, then compute the proposed row-space/active-face certificate on a small two-cohort example from §6 primitives.
Downgrade the Rambachan-Roth claim from strict tightening to a different TV sensitivity class, or prove a same-class tightening with the same pretrend calibration and target.

For flagship viability, replace the routine aggregate TV dual-norm theorem with a genuine cohort-level sharpness theorem against named staggered-adoption sensitivity workflows and location-level comparator anchors.

recommended_next_step: Reject this v1 for the flagship target; a viable retry must fix Theorem 2's boundary case and rebuild Conjecture 3 as a dimensionally valid, exhibited cohort-to-aggregate TV certificate.
